rationale: Y independently investigated and refuted X's headline 'major' finding (put_req_chunk never incremented), citing a framework-managed counter pattern shared with sibling processors — a strong signal X's claim is a false positive from an incomplete grep. Y also lands closer to a real GT High-severity concern (legacy MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer checkpoints mis-instantiating as v2) via its load_weights fallback/inferred_channels finding, while X's five findings mostly miss the substantive GT issues (legacy tokenizer bug, cumulative/delta rewind, ceil/floor codes-length bug, WER raw-bytes fallback, vendoring headers). Both share the test-coverage gap for the new streaming processor. X is slightly more directive/actionable with explicit fix snippets, but Y's higher-confidence, better-calibrated findings and self-correcting rigor outweigh that.
